prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, commonly used for stock price forecasting. For 18 calendar days, approximately 13 trading days were used. We assumed an annualized drift of 8% (daily drift of approximately 0.000317) and an annualized volatility of 25% (daily volatility of approximately 0.01575), which are typical long-term parameters for individual stocks. The point estimate is the median of the log-normal distribution, representing the most likely outcome under these assumptions. The confidence intervals are calculated by determining the price range within which the price is expected to fall with the given probability, based on the assumed volatility over 13 trading days. Future stock prices are inherently uncertain, and this prediction is for informational purposes only and not financial advice.
